metadata.title: Nail Seitai AI | Medical-Evidence-Based Nail Disease Screening
metadata.description: Combining AI image analysis with conversational health screening to suggest personalized nail care advice. For concerning symptoms, we recommend consulting a dermatologist.

hero.title: AI Image Screening for Nail Health
hero.subtitle: Non-invasive early detection of nail diseases, supported by medical evidence

evidence.title: AI Diagnosis Backed by Scientific Evidence
evidence.subtitle: Our service is built referencing publicly available academic nail disease image datasets and clinical guidelines from dermatological associations.

items.label: Nail Condition Assessment
items.detail: AI built referencing publicly available academic nail disease image datasets analyzes uploaded nail photos.

items.label: Comprehensive Lifestyle Evaluation
items.detail: Sleep, diet, exercise, stress and other lifestyle factors related to nail health are assessed through conversational screening.

items.label: Self-Care Recommendations
items.detail: We clearly explain square-off cutting techniques and guidance on when to see a doctor based on your nail condition.

card1.title: Benefits of Nail Seitai
card1.desc: By correcting nail shape and growth direction, nail seitai prevents ingrown nails and other issues, improving the overall balance of the foot and significantly enhancing daily comfort.

card2.title: Sports Performance Enhancement
card2.desc: Proper nail care is directly linked to athletic performance. You can expect improved grip strength, better running stability, and the ability to compete without pain.

card3.title: Foot Care & Callus Removal
card3.desc: Expert care for calluses, corns, and cracked heels based on professional manuals. Check our treatment guide and try AI diagnosis.

conditions.title: Detectable Nail Conditions
conditions.subtitle: Use this tool to self-check for the following nail conditions. For any concerning symptoms, we recommend consulting a dermatologist.

items.name: Ingrown / Pincer Nail
items.desc: Excessive lateral curvature of the nail plate (pincer nail), or the nail edge piercing the lateral nail fold causing inflammation and granulation (ingrown nail). Research links it to hallux valgus and gait patterns.

items.name: Over-trimming
items.desc: Cutting the nail edges too short causes soft tissue to rise from ground-reaction force during walking, becoming a direct trigger for ingrown nails. The Japanese Dermatological Association strongly recommends preventive guidance.

items.name: Onychomycosis
items.desc: Fungal infection by dermatophytes. Nails become white–yellow, thickened and brittle. Does not heal naturally; dermatological treatment with oral antifungals is necessary.

items.name: Paronychia
items.desc: Inflammation/infection of the nail fold. Acute type caused by Staphylococcus aureus (pus formation); chronic type by Candida (green nail). Common in people who frequently work with water.

items.name: Melanonychia
items.desc: A longitudinal black band in the nail. Usually benign melanin pigmentation, but differentiation from acral lentiginous melanoma (malignant tumor) is essential. More common as physiological pigmentation in people of color.

items.name: Other Nail Disorders
items.desc: Nail dystrophy (deformity/opacity), onycholysis (nail detachment from bed), clubbing (sign of lung/heart disease), psoriatic pitting. Can also be indicators of systemic diseases.

health.title: Importance of Healthy Nails
health.description: Nails are called the 'barometer of health,' reflecting your body's condition. Noticing changes in color, shape, and thickness leads to early health management.

merit.title: Benefits of Nail Seitai
merit.description: Adjusting nail shape and growth direction improves fingertip circulation and overall body balance. Also effective for preventing ingrown and deformed nails.

sports.title: Sports Performance Enhancement
sports.description: Proper nail care directly affects an athlete's grip and push-off power. Maximize performance with sport-specific nail seitai.

step1.title: Photo Upload
step1.description: Take a photo of your nails and upload it. Please photograph in good lighting with your entire nail visible.

step2.title: AI Image Analysis
step2.description: AI analyzes the uploaded nail image and combines it with lifestyle data gathered through conversation for a comprehensive nail condition assessment.

step3.title: Care Advice
step3.description: We suggest care methods tailored to your nail condition, including square-off cutting techniques and guidance on when to see a doctor.

squareOff.title: The Foundation of Ingrown Nail Prevention: Correct Nail Trimming
squareOff.subtitle: Square-off cut procedure based on the Japanese Dermatological Association 'Dermatology Q&A'

steps.title: Cut Straight Across
steps.desc: Cut or file the nail tip in a straight line perpendicular to the skin. Leave a small white portion; aim for the same height as or 1mm longer than the fingertip skin.
steps.note: Trim after bathing when nails are soft to prevent splitting

steps.title: Gently Round the Corners
steps.desc: Use a nail file to slightly round the corners. The key is keeping the straight central section clearly intact. Over-filing corners negates the ingrown nail prevention effect.
steps.note: 'Square-off' complete. Ideally the nail edge sits on top of surrounding skin

steps.title: Regular Maintenance
steps.desc: Nails grow ~3mm per month. Over-growth increases compression inside shoes, raising ingrown nail risk. Regular care 1–2 times per month maintains the ideal length.
steps.note: Check the cut edge after trimming. Smooth with a file if jagged

squareOff.warning: If the nail is already embedded with inflammation or granulation, do not attempt self-treatment. Visit a dermatologist or foot care clinic immediately.

dataCollection.title: Your Data Helps Improve Our Service
dataCollection.desc: Accumulating nail images and interview data helps improve the quality of AI analysis. All data is properly managed and used without linking to personal information.
dataCollection.privacy: Diagnostic data is not linked to personal information. Images are deleted within 24 hours of diagnosis.

disclaimer.title: Important Medical Notice
disclaimer.text: This service is not a medical device under Japanese pharmaceutical law and does not provide definitive diagnoses. It is provided as a screening tool to help recommend appropriate timing for medical consultation.
disclaimer.urgent: If you notice melanonychia (black vertical lines) or rapid discoloration, please visit a dermatologist (skin malignancy specialist) urgently. Early detection of malignant melanoma is critical for prognosis.

about.title: About Nail Care
about.subtitle: Your nails are the barometer of your overall health

hero.title: Reading Health Clues from Nail Changes
hero.description: Changes in nail morphology, color, and structure can reflect your lifestyle habits and overall health. Regular nail self-checks are the first step in health management.

anatomy.subtitle: The nail is composed of 4 major structures

parts.name: Nail Plate
parts.desc: The nail body — a hard semi-transparent plate of stacked keratin protein. A healthy nail is pink, lustrous, and uniformly thick.

parts.name: Nail Bed
parts.desc: The skin beneath the nail plate, rich in capillaries — the source of the nail's pink color. Discoloration (blue/yellow/brown) is an important indicator of systemic status.

parts.name: Nail Matrix
parts.desc: Where stem cells producing the nail plate are concentrated. The 'lunula' (white semicircle) at the nail base is the tip of the matrix. Damage here causes nail deformity.

parts.name: Nail Fold
parts.desc: Skin folds surrounding the nail base and sides. The cuticle acts as a barrier against bacteria and fungi. In chronic paronychia, the cuticle disappears.

systemic.subtitle: 87% of systemic diseases reportedly show symptoms in the nails (research survey)

items.note: Peripheral neuropathy delays symptom awareness, making deterioration more likely.
Low hemoglobin makes the nail bed pale. Improves with iron supplementation.
Indicator of chronic hypoxia. Also seen in lung cancer and COPD.
Nail symptoms may precede psoriatic arthritis. Important for early diagnosis.

skinColor.sectionTitle: Skin of Color — Diagnostic Differences by Skin Tone
skinColor.subtitle: Special care is needed in diagnosing nail disorders in people of color
skinColor.description: According to Fitzpatrick skin type, the appearance of nail diseases varies greatly. In patients with Skin of Color (SOC), inflammatory erythema is less visible, presenting instead as purple or gray discoloration.

melanonychia.title: Differentiation of Melanonychia is Especially Critical
melanonychia.desc: In people of color, benign melanin pigmentation is commonly seen in multiple nails. However, differentiating it from acral lentiginous melanoma (malignant tumor) with the same appearance is essential, and AI misclassification risk must always be factored into the design.

skinColor.advice: If your skin tone is darker, use AI diagnosis results as reference while always confirming with a dermatologist.

importance.sectionTitle: How Nail Health Affects the Whole Body

items.title: Cardiovascular Impact
items.description: Nail color and shape reflect blood circulation. Bluish nails may indicate oxygen deficiency; yellow nails may signal liver issues.

items.title: Nutritional Indicator
items.description: Ridges and brittleness signal nutritional deficiency — especially B12, iron, and zinc.

items.title: Stress & Fatigue Barometer
items.description: Intense stress and overwork can slow nail growth and cause deformation. Nail condition reveals mental and physical exhaustion.

items.title: Thyroid & Hormonal Function
items.description: Thyroid conditions and hormonal imbalances affect nail texture and growth patterns. Regular monitoring is important.

history.description: Nail care history dates back to ancient civilizations. Ancient Egyptians used nail color to indicate social status; Chinese traditional medicine used nail condition for diagnosis. In Japan, it evolved uniquely as 'Nail Seitai,' now established as a scientifically grounded care method.

timeline.event: Nail care begins in ancient Egypt
Nail diagnosis incorporated into Chinese traditional medicine
Japan's unique nail care culture develops
AI-based nail disease analysis research advances globally
AI services combining nail care and lifestyle assessment become mainstream

science.description: Modern research has reported numerous correlations between nail condition and overall health.

stats.label: of diseases show symptoms in the nails (research survey)
nails grow approx. 3mm per month, recording health history

nailSeitai.title: About Nail Seitai
nailSeitai.subtitle: Body improvement starting from your nails

hero.title: Maximize Your Performance with Nail Seitai
hero.description: Ingrown nails are a systemic problem closely related to foot skeletal structure and gait. Understanding the causes and taking a scientific approach helps address the root issue.

biomechanics.subtitle: Key causes of ingrown nails identified through clinical research
biomechanics.description: Nails serve to push down the ground-reaction force applied to the toe pad during walking. When this upward pressure is insufficient — such as in floating toes (where toes don't contact the ground) — the nail's natural tendency to curl prevails, advancing the ingrown nail.

factors.title: Foot Skeletal Deformity
factors.desc: Hallux valgus, flat foot, and other structural deformities promote lateral curvature of the nail plate. Bone hypertrophy at the distal phalanx base is one cause of pincer nail.

factors.title: Floating Toes & Gait
factors.desc: When toes don't contact the ground (floating toes), ground-reaction force isn't transmitted to the nail and the nail's curling tendency can't be suppressed. Learning proper gait is the key to prevention.

factors.title: Over-trimming & Improper Cutting
factors.desc: Cutting the nail's lateral edges too short allows soft tissue to rise during walking, and the growing nail drives directly into it. The Japanese Dermatological Association strongly recommends preventive guidance.

factors.title: Shoe Compression
factors.desc: Narrow-toed shoes and high heels compress the nail plate laterally, causing chronic deformity. Choosing appropriate footwear directly contributes to long-term prevention.

what.description: Nail Seitai is a professional practice that adjusts nail shape and growth direction to maximize fingertip function and improve overall performance. Unlike cosmetic nail care, its primary purpose is physical function improvement.

features.title: Nail Shape Optimization
features.description: We propose and adjust the optimal nail shape and length based on your sport or profession.

features.title: Ingrown & Deformed Nail Correction
features.description: We safely correct ingrown and deformed nails that cause pain and discomfort.

features.title: Improved Circulation
features.description: Nail bed massage and nail correction promote blood circulation, sharpening fingertip sensitivity.

features.title: Preventive Care
features.description: Regular maintenance prevents nail problems before they occur, maintaining long-term health.

treatments.subtitle: A stepwise approach based on severity

items.title: Square-off Cut + Foot Care
items.desc: Switch to proper nail trimming (square-off) combined with moisturizing care and toe stretching. Most early ingrown nails can be resolved at this stage.

items.title: Superelastic Wire Correction
items.desc: A representative conservative treatment at clinics. A shape-memory alloy wire is attached to the nail tip and uses its restoring force to gradually correct the curvature. Low pain, with rapid pain-relief effect expected.

items.title: Light-curing Resin Nail Extension
items.desc: Applied when there is insufficient nail for wire correction due to over-trimming. Light-curing or acrylic resin artificially extends and shapes the nail to resolve the embedding. Often combined with wire correction.

items.title: Urgent Visit to Dermatology or Foot Care Clinic
items.desc: Redness, swelling, heat, or granulation around the nail strongly suggests bacterial secondary infection (soft tissue infection). Self-treatment worsens symptoms — seek specialist care immediately.

sports.description: For athletes, nail condition is a crucial factor that directly affects performance.

items.benefit: Optimizing nail length and shape improves push-off power and ground contact feel.
Fingertip sensation for controlling balls improves, enhancing grip and precision.
Prevent nail injuries during grappling while maximizing grip strength and fingertip power.
Shaping nails to minimize resistance when pulling water increases propulsion.

items.title: Strict Square-off Cut
items.desc: Cut nails straight across and round the corners slightly. The most effective ingrown nail prevention method. Recommended by the Japanese Dermatological Association.

items.title: Toe Muscle Strengthening
items.desc: Towel-gathering exercises (grasping a towel with toes) strengthen toe muscles and improve floating toes.

items.title: Proper Footwear
items.desc: Choose shoes with about 1cm of space at the toe and the right width. Avoid high heels and narrow-toed shoes.

items.title: Correct Gait
items.desc: Be mindful of using the full pad of each toe to push off the ground. Land on the heel and push off with the big toe.

items.name: Client A (Marathon Runner, 40s)
items.before: Nails turned black and eventually fell off after every 10km run
items.after: Zero nail problems after 3 nail seitai sessions. Personal best improved by 3 minutes

items.name: Client B (Basketball Player, 20s)

items.after: Completely resolved after 2 months of correction treatment. Play quality improved

items.name: Client C (Desk Worker, 30s female)

items.after: Nail strength improved with dietary guidance and regular care. Work efficiency increased

items.title: Proven Track Record

judo.sectionTitle: Finger Strength in Martial Arts & Nail Seitai
judo.description: In judo and jiu-jitsu, 'finger force'—the ability to grip and control—is distinct from overall grip strength. Nail Seitai scientifically supports this by optimizing fingertip sensation and function.
judo.evidence: Research shows that competitive judo performance correlates more strongly with fingertip sensory precision than with overall grip strength. Ingrown nails and nail deformities impair nerve conduction at the fingertip, disrupting fine motor control. Nail Seitai improves circulation and nerve function around the nail, enhancing tactile sensitivity and the ability to sense an opponent's movements.

items.title: Finger Force vs. Grip Strength
items.desc: Grip strength measures overall hand force, while finger force refers to the precise 'hook, pull, push' action of individual fingers. The effectiveness of judo's gripping techniques depends directly on finger force.

items.title: Nail Condition & Nerve Conduction
items.desc: The nail bed contains abundant Merkel cells (tactile receptors). Chronic pressure from ingrown nails inhibits these receptors, reducing fingertip sensory precision.

items.title: Immediate Effects of Nail Seitai
items.desc: Blood circulation around the nail improves immediately after treatment, and fingertip sensation becomes sharper. Many athletes notice a change in their grip feel on the same day.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.desc: We carefully listen to your nail condition, concerns, and lifestyle including sports and occupation.

steps.title: Nail Assessment
steps.desc: We examine nail shape, color, and thickness in detail using specialized tools to identify issues.

steps.title: Treatment Plan
steps.desc: We propose and explain the optimal treatment plan tailored to your goals and nail condition.

steps.title: Nail Seitai Treatment
steps.desc: We carefully perform nail shaping, correction, and care. There is minimal pain during treatment.

steps.title: Aftercare Guidance
steps.desc: We explain self-care methods and the recommended schedule for the next session to maintain results.

gallery.description: See our real treatment cases. A record of clients who have restored beautiful, healthy nails with care tailored to their condition.

items.question: Is Nail Seitai painful?
items.answer: There is virtually no pain. Correction involves attaching a flexible wire to the nail, and most clients find relief from pain immediately. If inflammation or infection is present, we recommend medical treatment first.

items.question: How many sessions are needed?
items.answer: It depends on severity. Mild cases typically require 1–3 sessions, moderate cases 3–6 sessions. Lasting improvement also involves self-care and correcting walking habits alongside treatment.

items.question: Can I receive treatment if I play sports?
items.answer: Absolutely—we especially recommend it for athletes. Treatment is available anytime except just before a match or training. We also optimize nail shape for specific sports.

items.question: Can I play sports right after treatment?
items.answer: In most cases, yes. However, after wire correction, intense contact sports may dislodge the wire. Please consult your practitioner.

items.question: Can I cut my nails at home?
items.answer: Yes, correct nail trimming is important self-care. We will instruct you on the proper technique (square-off cutting) during your visit. Deep cutting is strictly avoided.

hero.title: Better Nails, Better Performance
hero.subtitle: Movement changes from the tips of your toes outward. The potential of nail correction therapy.

intro.title: What is Nail Seitai?
intro.description: Nail deformities directly affect ground contact angle, plantar pressure distribution, and load alignment through the big toe joint. Problems like ingrown nails, over-trimming, and floating nails cause postural imbalance, pelvic tilt, and asymmetry in running form. No matter how much you train your muscles, an 'input error' at the foot prevents you from reaching your true potential.

cards.title: Ground Contact
cards.desc: When toes contact the ground correctly, the stability and propulsion of each step improves.

cards.title: Posture
cards.desc: Plantar load balance is restored, naturally correcting pelvic and spinal tilt.

cards.title: Power Output
cards.desc: Muscle force transmits correctly, allowing explosive speed and endurance to reach their true potential.

sports.title: Performance Gains by Sport
sports.subtitle: How nail condition affects performance varies depending on the sport.

cards.title: Soccer & Futsal
cards.description: Every action — kicking, planting, changing direction — begins with toe contact. Corrected nails stabilize load through the ball of the foot, improving explosive acceleration and sharp cuts.

cards.title: Basketball
cards.description: Toe stability is essential for stop-and-go and rapid direction changes. Balanced plantar loading improves jump takeoff power and distributes landing impact more effectively.

cards.title: Judo & Jiu-Jitsu [Deep Dive]
cards.description: Beyond grip strength, there is a separate attribute called 'finger force.' Proper nail care allows finger pads to sink deeply into the opponent's gi, dramatically improving holding power during pulling, breaking balance, and throwing. With ingrown or over-trimmed nails, force can't reach the fingertips — leaving you unable to control the opponent even with strong hands.

cards.title: Baseball & Softball
cards.description: Fingertip feel when pitching and bat grip when hitting are directly linked to nail condition. Unstable fingertips from over-trimming cause inconsistent release points and reduced control.

cards.title: Track & Field (Sprints)
cards.description: How you use your toes at the start and through the acceleration phase can make or break your race. Nail correction sharpens toe contact awareness and improves your first-step drive.

cards.title: Tennis & Badminton
cards.description: Footwork agility is built on toe grip. Fingernail condition also affects racket grip during serves and smashes.

mechanism.title: The Body's Chain Reaction
mechanism.subtitle: Nail condition doesn't stop at the foot — it cascades through your entire body.

care.title: Daily Care Tips
care.subtitle: Consistent self-care alongside professional treatment keeps your nails healthy long-term.

cards.title: Nail Length
cards.desc: Trim to the same level as your fingertip. Over-cutting destabilizes the toe — avoid it.

cards.title: Cutting Technique
cards.desc: Use a straight square-off cut, leaving the corners. Rounding the edges increases ingrown nail risk.

cards.title: Moisturize
cards.desc: Care for the skin at the nail root and sides with cream or oil. Dryness causes cracking and chipping.

cards.title: Weekly Check
cards.desc: Inspect your toes once a week. Catch discoloration, deformity, or pain early.

cta.title: Diagnose Your Nail Health with AI
cta.description: One photo of your nails and a quick questionnaire is all it takes to analyze your toe and nail condition and assess your performance risk.

jujutsu.heroTitle: Change Your Nails, Change Your Jiu-Jitsu Body Mechanics!
jujutsu.heroSub: How Nail Seitai changed the core of competitive jiu-jitsu
jujutsu.intervieweeRole: SENDAI Jiu-Jitsu Academy Head Instructor / Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu Black Belt 1st Dan
jujutsu.intro: As head of the jiu-jitsu academy, Noriyuki Tsunoda collaborated on systematizing the Nail Seitai methodology for jiu-jitsu — understanding the real struggles of his athletes and the core of their movement. What emerged was a previously unexplored territory: 'Change your nails, and your entire jiu-jitsu body mechanics change.'

qa.q: What did you notice when you first tried Nail Seitai?
qa.a: The first thing I realized was how little I knew about nails. I'd studied body mechanics and training extensively, but I'd barely thought about nails. Once I started learning, I discovered that nails are deeply tied to fingertip sensation, balance, and force transmission — playing a far more important role than I'd imagined. They affect everything from posture and stability to athletic performance. These small structures are fundamental to how the whole body functions.

qa.q: Why did you decide to collaborate on systematizing this?
qa.a: Simply because I found it fascinating. I also wanted to test it personally — to see whether strengthening my nails would actually improve my grip strength.

qa.q: What is the 'athlete-only sensation' that became central to the system?
qa.a: Matches are won and lost by razor-thin margins. Small differences accumulate to change results. I believe that nails are one of those marginal factors — one component of closing that gap.

qa.q: What changes did you experience after Nail Seitai treatment?
qa.a: The nail on my little toe was nearly gone, and the toe itself barely moved. After the nail was rebuilt, my little toe actually moved properly for the first time. When I tried to brace in a standing position, I could feel the grip strength in my feet had genuinely increased. And honestly, just seeing polished nails gave me a boost of motivation.

qa.q: How do you think Nail Seitai will influence the future of jiu-jitsu in Miyagi?
qa.a: Conditioning your nails before a big competition could become a standard part of match preparation — a culture of nail strengthening spreading through the sport. It's a small element, but I believe it's a meaningful one for supporting performance.

jujutsu.quote: I realized that small nails support the body and its performance
jujutsu.foundationTitle: Walking is the beginning of all movement

foundationQa.a: Feet are the body's roots. When sensory information from the soles and nails to the brain is disrupted, posture and movement judgments go awry — leading to fatigue, instability, and injury.
Fatigue and poor posture often stem not from muscle weakness, but from information errors at the extremities. Caring for nails and soles helps stabilize the entire body.
Nails play a crucial role in transmitting force and delivering sensation to the brain. Reconsidering how nails contact the ground can elevate the quality of stability and movement from the feet up.

foundationQa.q: The relationship between walking and feet
foundationQa.a: Walking is the most fundamental full-body movement. When toes, soles, and nails work correctly, accurate information reaches the brain from the extremities, stabilizing center of gravity and movement.

jujutsu.profile: Born 1985 in Tagajo City, Miyagi. Progressed through karate and judo before specializing in jiu-jitsu. Joined AXIS Jiu-Jitsu Academy in 2005. After working in consulting, founded SENDAI Jiu-Jitsu Academy in 2015. Earned black belt in 2018. Currently serves as external coach for Olympic athletes. Extensive coaching career with numerous achievements.

kickboxing.heroTitle: A Pro Kickboxer on the Importance of Your Foundation
kickboxing.heroSub: When your foundation changes, your movement changes
kickboxing.missionTitle: The Nail Seitai Approach: Aligning the Body from Nails, Toes & Soles
kickboxing.missionBody: Nail Seitai is an approach to aligning the whole body starting from the extremities — the nails, toes, and soles. The body is structured like a tree: the trunk and branches (core and muscles) only function correctly when the roots (foot structure) work properly. Nail Seitai focuses on this root structure. Nails in particular are critical sensory elements that control the direction of ground-contact forces and how toes are used. Based on nail biomechanics principles, adjusting nail shape, angle, and pressure distribution restores coordination between toes and soles.

pillars.label: Nail Input
pillars.desc: Ground-contact information from the extremities reaches the brain, transforming the quality of motor commands.

pillars.label: Nail Output
pillars.desc: Nail tip angle and hardness determine the final output of push, kick, and grip.

pillars.label: Nail Training
pillars.desc: Re-inputting fingertip sensory information to the nervous system elevates brain-body coordination and unlocks fundamental gains in force transmission, stability, and performance.

qa.q: What was your struggle before treatment?
qa.a: My stance was unstable — I had a feeling of losing power. I was training my lower body hard, but something felt misaligned.

qa.q: How did you change after treatment?
qa.a: I could feel my toes gripping the ground, and my axis stopped wobbling. It felt like my body connected from the extremities inward.

qa.q: What did you notice when you saw your foot AI data?
qa.a: Force transmits directly into technique. I had no idea my feet and nails mattered this much. 'When your foundation changes, your movement changes.'

qa.q: How did you feel seeing the data?
qa.a: Honestly, I was shocked. Things like 'my weight distribution is this different left to right' or 'I'm using my feet less than I thought' were showing up as numbers. Parts I thought I was executing correctly by feel — seeing them in data made everything click.

qa.q: What changes came from combining it with Nail Seitai?
qa.a: After Nail Seitai treatment, when I measured with the insole again, I could see the center-of-gravity fluctuation had decreased. Specifically — stance stability, less pivot foot wobble, and less fatigue during matches — these clearly changed.

s2.title: A Pro Kickboxer on the Importance of Your Foundation

qa.q: What was your struggle before treatment?
qa.a: My stance was unstable — I had a feeling of losing power. I was training my lower body hard, but something felt misaligned.

qa.q: How did you change after treatment?
qa.a: I could feel my toes gripping the ground, and my axis stopped wobbling. It felt like my body connected from the extremities inward.

qa.q: What did you notice when you saw your foot AI data?
qa.a: Force transmits directly into technique. I had no idea my feet and nails mattered this much. 'When your foundation changes, your movement changes.'

qa.q: How did you feel seeing the data?
qa.a: Honestly, I was shocked. Things like 'my weight distribution is this different left to right' or 'I'm using my feet less than I thought' were showing up as numbers. Parts I thought I was executing correctly by feel — seeing them in data made everything click.

s3.title: The Nail Seitai Approach: Aligning the Body from Nails, Toes & Soles
s3.body: Nail Seitai is an approach to aligning the whole body starting from the extremities — the nails, toes, and soles. The body is structured like a tree: the trunk and branches (core and muscles) only function correctly when the roots (foot structure) work properly. Nail Seitai focuses on this root structure. Nails in particular are critical sensory elements that control the direction of ground-contact forces and how toes are used.

pillars.label: Nail Input
pillars.desc: Ground-contact information from the extremities reaches the brain, transforming the quality of motor commands.

pillars.label: Nail Output
pillars.desc: Nail tip angle and hardness determine the final output of push, kick, and grip.

pillars.label: Nail Training
pillars.desc: Re-inputting fingertip sensory information to the nervous system elevates brain-body coordination and unlocks fundamental gains in force transmission, stability, and performance.

s4.title: Change Your Nails, Change Your Jiu-Jitsu Body Mechanics!

qa.q: What did you notice when you first tried Nail Seitai?
qa.a: The first thing I realized was how little I knew about nails. I'd studied body mechanics and training extensively, but I'd barely thought about nails. Once I started learning, I discovered that nails are deeply tied to fingertip sensation, balance, and force transmission.

qa.q: Why did you decide to collaborate on systematizing this?
qa.a: Simply because I found it fascinating. I also wanted to test it personally — to see whether strengthening my nails would actually improve my grip strength.

qa.q: What is the 'athlete-only sensation' that became central to the system?
qa.a: Matches are won and lost by razor-thin margins. Small differences accumulate to change results. I believe that nails are one of those marginal factors — one component of closing that gap.

qa.q: What changes did you experience after Nail Seitai treatment?
qa.a: The nail on my little toe was nearly gone, and the toe itself barely moved. After the nail was rebuilt, my little toe actually moved properly for the first time. I could feel the grip strength in my feet had genuinely increased.

qa.q: How do you think Nail Seitai will influence the future of jiu-jitsu in Miyagi?
qa.a: Conditioning your nails before a big competition could become a standard part of match preparation — a culture of nail strengthening spreading through the sport.

s4.profile: Born 1985 in Tagajo City, Miyagi. Progressed through karate and judo before specializing in jiu-jitsu. Joined AXIS Jiu-Jitsu Academy in 2005. Founded SENDAI Jiu-Jitsu Academy in 2015. Earned black belt in 2018.

s5.title: Nail Seitai® Evidence Data — 3 Case Studies
s5.subtitle: Visualizing posture and movement changes with objective measurement data

cases.label: Case 1 · Ankle Stability
cases.before: Repeated ankle sprains with instability during lateral movements. Balance impairment from insufficient toe ground contact.
cases.after: Significant improvement in ankle stability through Nail Seitai and insole support. No re-injury at 3-month follow-up.

cases.label: Case 2 · Trip Prevention
cases.before: Frequent stumbling during walking. Toes unable to grip the ground, resulting in a toe-dragging gait pattern.
cases.after: Toe grip restored through Nail Seitai. Toe dragging eliminated. Zero stumbles during the follow-up period.

cases.label: Case 3 · Walking Stability
cases.before: Sensing insole detected left-right center-of-gravity imbalance. Chronic knee pain during extended walking.
cases.after: Center of gravity normalized after nail correction. Knee pain resolved and walking efficiency measurably improved.

items.title: Practitioner Training Program
items.desc: Launched a Nail Seitai practitioner training curriculum integrating biomechanics, sports science, and clinical nail care.

items.title: Regional Sports Club Partnerships
items.desc: Partnered with sports clubs across Miyagi Prefecture to provide ongoing athlete support and performance optimization services.

items.title: AI Diagnosis & Sensing Insole Integration
items.desc: Released an AI nail diagnosis system. Integrated with NEC sensing insole technology for data-driven nail care assessment.

s7.title: Walking Is the Beginning of All Movement

qa.q: Why are feet so important?
qa.a: Feet are the body's roots. When sensory information from the soles and nails to the brain is disrupted, posture and movement judgments go awry — leading to fatigue, instability, and injury.

qa.q: What causes dysfunction?
qa.a: Fatigue and poor posture often stem not from muscle weakness, but from information errors at the extremities. Caring for nails and soles helps stabilize the entire body.

qa.q: The potential of nails
qa.a: Nails play a crucial role in transmitting force and delivering sensation to the brain. Reconsidering how nails contact the ground can elevate the quality of stability and movement from the feet up.

qa.q: The relationship between walking and feet
qa.a: Walking is the most fundamental full-body movement. When toes, soles, and nails work correctly, accurate information reaches the brain from the extremities, stabilizing center of gravity and movement.

s7.profile: Certified Nail Seitai instructor specializing in foot biomechanics and nail care for daily health and sports performance. Dedicated to spreading the science of nail health through education and practice.

s8.title: Human Acupuncture Clinic
s8.body: Human Acupuncture Clinic is a partner clinic specializing in the fusion of traditional acupuncture with the Nail Seitai method. Based in Miyagi Prefecture, it provides evidence-based treatments for athletes and anyone seeking whole-body optimization.
s8.pointsTitle: 3 Key Strengths of Human Acupuncture Clinic
points: Sports Performance Optimization: Combining acupuncture with nail biomechanics to address the root causes of performance decline from the ground up.
Tradition Meets Technology: Integrating time-honored acupuncture techniques with modern data measurement via NEC sensing insoles.
Evidence-Based Care: All treatments are tracked with objective data, providing measurable outcomes and personalized care plans.
